title: Facial pressure ENT review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact adult sinusitis assessment section supports onset, facial pain or pressure context, nasal symptoms, associated features, and relevant negatives without diagnosing sinusitis or bacterial infection.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 1A–1B — acute rhinosinusitis distinction and clinical diagnosis context

red_flags.text: Lists high fever, frontal or periorbital swelling, severe headache, meningeal or mental-status change, visual change, and persistent unilateral or bloody symptoms.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA red-flag section supports clinician concern, safety-net documentation, and an entered plan only when assessed; it does not infer orbital, neurological, or systemic complication.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists high fever, frontal or periorbital swelling, severe headache, meningeal or mental-status change, visual change, and persistent unilateral or bloody symptoms.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis

examination.text: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment and review of already available imaging or specialist information without interpreting CT or attributing pressure to sinus disease.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps

sources_used.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation

sources_used.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis

source_limitations: Site, laterality, duration, nasal symptoms, headache, vision, dental symptoms, fever, neurological features, examination, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
